Geocoding

Jennifer LaFleur St. Louis Post-Dispatch May 2000
Demographics & CensusTechnology

Using mapping technology like ArcView GIS for geocoding addresses, this report describes processes undertaken in various investigations, including the examination of building permits versus landslide-...

Using mapping technology like ArcView GIS for geocoding addresses, this report describes processes undertaken in various investigations, including the examination of building permits versus landslide-prone zones. Geocoding translates addresses into latitudes and longitudes, enabling incidents to be geospatially analyzed against trends, demonstrating powerful visual tools in journalism. LaFleur shares tips on using software tools effectively and highlights the importance of clean, accurate location data to construct impactful narrative maps.
